texto: We cultivate our feelings the way we cultivate a garden: we can't entirely prevent weeds from coming up, but we can take care to remove them before they do much harm.
explicacao: Citação de Phillip Cary sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “We cultivate our feelings the way we cultivate a garden: we can't entirely prevent weeds from com…” articula reflexao, discipline, feelings em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como feelings e virtue.

frase_original: We cultivate our feelings the way we cultivate a garden: we can't entirely prevent weeds from coming up, but we can take care to remove them before they do much harm.

texto: Every time we turn to Christ in faith it is like a moment of Sabbath, a little foretaste of eternal rest and glory. The gift of that moment lies not in what we do but what we receive. It is the holy time set aside to receive the greatest gift of God ever has to give, which is himself, in his own beloved Son.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Phillip Cary: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Every time we turn to Christ in faith it is like a moment of Sabbath, a little foretaste of etern…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, faith, rest pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de rest e sabbath.

frase_original: Every time we turn to Christ in faith it is like a moment of Sabbath, a little foretaste of eternal rest and glory. The gift of that moment lies not in what we do but what we receive. It is the holy time set aside to receive the greatest gift of God ever has to give, which is himself, in his own beloved Son.
